set_fields.description: Two-phase orchestration loop on backlog items: implement then review, repeat until reviewer approves.nn## DesignnnForge binds to a backlog epic/task. Each cycle:n1. IMPLEMENT: fresh session, task description + last review feedback injected. Agent works, calls forge_done.n2. REVIEW: same session (reviewer sees impl context), reviewer prompt injected. Reviewer calls forge_review.n   - reject + feedback -> saved in state.json, new session, back to IMPLEMENTn   - complete -> backlog item closed, loop endsnn## Key constraintsn- Only user starts forge (no forge_start tool)n- Tools dynamically toggled via setActiveTools: forge_done during IMPLEMENTING, forge_review during REVIEWING, neither when inactiven- Implementer uses current mode + model (no config)n- Reviewer always uses reviewer prompt/mode; model overridable via /forge setn- Implementer can comment on backlog item but CANNOT close it (intercepted via tool_call event)n- Only reviewer can close the item (via forge_review complete verdict)n- Review feedback returned through forge_review tool, stored in state.json, NOT as backlog commentn- Reviewer can update backlog item description/fieldsnn## Staten```typescriptninterface ForgeState {n  taskId: string;n  phase: 'implementing' | 'reviewing';n  cycle: number;n  status: 'active' | 'paused' | 'completed';n  reviewer: { model?: string };n  lastFeedback?: string;n  startedAt: string;n  completedAt?: string;n}n```nn## Filesn.pi/forge/<task-id>/state.jsonnn## Commandsn/forge start <task-id>n/forge stopn/forge resume <task-id>n/forge statusn/forge set <task-id> reviewer model <id>n/forge cancel <task-id>
